reasoning: As of early 2026, US relations with the Taliban (the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan) are extremely hostile and moving further away from normalization, not closer. In March 2026, the US officially designated Afghanistan as a "State Sponsor of Wrongful Detention," citing the Taliban's continued use of terrorist tactics and hostage diplomacy. Furthermore, the US has recently ended foreign assistance and ended efforts to relocate/resettle Afghan allies. Open conflict has also broken out between Afghanistan and Pakistan. Historically, the US takes decades to recognize hostile regimes that seize power by force (e.g., Vietnam, PRC, Cuba, Iran). Given the active hostility, the continued harbor of terrorists, and severe human rights regressions by the Taliban, it is highly implausible that the US will formally recognize the Islamic Emirate by the start of 2030. The community consensus was already very low in 2024 and recent 2026 developments push the probability to the minimum baseline threshold limit.
